full_prompt: Design the primary structure of a high-school-division CanSat benchmark. The CanSat must fit inside a 350 ml can envelope of outside diameter 66 mm and height 130 mm. Total mass including structure, electronics, and payload must not exceed 350 g (high-school class). The CanSat is deployed from a small model rocket at approximately 100 m altitude, descending on a parachute. Structural pass/fail: the chassis must survive a 15 G deployment shock in all three principal axes with no component displacement exceeding 2 mm. A touchdown ground-impact equivalent of 30 G (5 m/s descent into soft ground treated as a 30 G half-sine) must not produce plastic yield in the primary structure. The chassis must hold an antenna for 920 MHz (LoRa) or 430 MHz telemetry, and therefore must provide a non-conductive RF window or slot. Primary verification is linear static structural FEA at 15 G and 30 G, plus a modal analysis confirming first natural frequency above 80 Hz (to decouple from the small rocket motor's burn frequency). Vibration (modal) is a secondary gate. Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the CanSat, a mass budget, and a report listing total mass in grams, max bracket stress at 30 G in MPa, max component displacement at 15 G in mm, and first modal frequency in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design the primary structure of a high-school-division CanSat. Envelope: 350 ml can (66 mm OD x 130 mm height). Total mass <= 350 g. Deployed from a small model rocket at ~100 m altitude; descends on a parachute. Telemetry on 430 MHz or 920 MHz (LoRa) so the chassis must provide a non-conductive RF window/slot. First natural frequency >= 80 Hz (rocket motor burn decoupling).

Load cases.
- LC1 deployment shock X: 15 G axial.
- LC2 deployment shock Y: 15 G lateral.
- LC3 deployment shock Z: 15 G lateral. Max component displacement <= 2 mm under LC1-LC3.
- LC4 touchdown ground-impact equivalent: 30 G half-sine axial (5 m/s into soft ground) with no plastic yield in the primary structure.

prompt.title: High-school CanSat chassis
prompt.brief: Design a 350 ml can envelope CanSat, ≤ 350 g, survives 15 G deployment + 30 G touchdown, first mode ≥ 80 Hz.

material.typical: Al 6061-T6 sheet or PLA/ABS print + FR4 decks

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: No plastic yield at 30 G touchdown
